touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message sent to Sebastian Hernandez introducing call recording solution for CS teams. No response received.
Cold email to Sebastian highlighting ROI from coaching enablement for eCommerce support teams. No response.
Follow-up email with case study from similar eCommerce company showing 23% improvement in CSAT. No response.
LinkedIn message sharing recent webinar on CS coaching automation. No response received.
Email asking if call quality and agent coaching is a priority for Q2. No response.
Cold call attempt to Sebastian's direct line. Left voicemail mentioning eCommerce CS use case. No callback.
Email referencing voicemail and offering 15-minute intro to discuss CS team challenges. No response.

9-minute qualification call with Sebastian. Discussed current CS coaching process (manual, inconsistent). He mentioned team of ~20 agents handling eCommerce support. Interested in automation and call recording. Agreed to internal discussion with RevOps and leadership.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for coaching CS agents on customer calls?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly ad-hoc manager reviews, no systematic recording or feedback loop

questions_asked.question: Does this integrate with our existing CRM and support tools?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with major platforms and APIs for custom setups

next_steps: Sebastian to discuss internally with RevOps and Revenue leadership
Rep to send overview deck and pricing framework

touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt during prospecting. No answer, no voicemail left.
Follow-up email after qualification call. Sent overview deck and example coaching workflow. Sebastian responded thanking us and confirming internal meeting scheduled for next week.

questions_asked.question: Can you clarify the seat-based licensing vs usage-based pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Seat-based covers named users, unlimited recording/analysis per seat. Usage-based available for enterprise scale.

next_steps: Sebastian to meet with Nathan (SVP Revenue) and Amelia (RevOps)

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message during prospecting phase. No response received.
Cold call attempt. No answer, left brief voicemail. No callback.
Cold email with industry benchmark data on CS efficiency. No response.
Follow-up email checking in after internal meeting. Sebastian replied that team is interested and wants to move forward with demo. Mentioned Nathan (SVP Revenue) wants to see business case.
next_steps: Schedule demo for CS and RevOps team

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email after demo. Sebastian confirmed team loved the platform. Mentioned Amelia from RevOps will be key stakeholder for implementation. Asked about timeline to proposal.

22-minute proposal call with Sebastian. Walked through pricing for 17 seats with unlimited automation tier. Discussed implementation timeline (2-3 weeks). Sebastian mentioned budget constraints and asked about flexibility on pricing. Introduced Rowan from procurement who will handle contract.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the 17-seat count vs your ~20 agent team?
questions_asked.answer: Starting with core team, may expand later based on adoption

questions_asked.question: What's your standard payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: Net 30, annual prepay standard. Quarterly available for enterprise.

objections: List price feels high for our budget cycle, need to see if there's flexibility
next_steps: Sebastian to review proposal with Nathan
Schedule executive alignment call with Nathan

touches.ai_summary: 36-minute executive meeting with Nathan (SVP Revenue) and Sebastian. Meeting was rescheduled once. Nathan focused on business impact and ROI metrics. Concerned about team adoption and change management. Discussed integrations with existing revenue stack. Nathan generally supportive but wants to ensure CS team commitment. Asked about discount given budget timing.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical time to value for CS teams like ours?
questions_asked.answer: Most teams see measurable coaching improvements within 30-45 days post-implementation

questions_asked.question: Is there executive alignment on this being a priority initiative this quarter?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, CS efficiency is critical for us as we scale eCommerce support volume

questions_asked.question: What kind of pricing flexibility do you have given our Q2 budget constraints?
questions_asked.answer: We can work within your budget cycle - let me discuss options with leadership

objections: Need to ensure we're not adding another tool that doesn't get adopted by the CS team
next_steps: Rep to return with discounted pricing options
Sebastian to confirm CS team commitment and implementation owner

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message to Sebastian checking in on timeline. He responded positively, mentioned Nathan is on board and they're working through budget allocation.

11-minute pricing call with Sebastian. Presented revised pricing with 32% discount to hit budget target of $13K ACV. Sebastian appreciated the flexibility and thinks this will work. Mentioned Rowan needs to review final terms and contract language.

questions_asked.question: Is this discount locked for the first year or does pricing reset?
questions_asked.answer: Locked for first 12 months, renewal pricing typically within 10% of Year 1

next_steps: Send revised proposal to Sebastian and Rowan
Rowan to review contract and procurement requirements

touches.ai_summary: 25-minute pricing and contract call with Sebastian and Rowan. Walked through final proposal at $13K for 17 seats. Rowan asked detailed questions about contract terms, auto-renewal, data security, and SLAs. Sebastian confirmed this fits budget and has Nathan's approval. Discussed implementation plan with Amelia as technical lead. All parties aligned to move forward pending legal review.

questions_asked.question: What's the cancellation policy if we need to terminate early?
questions_asked.answer: 30-day notice required, pro-rated refund for unused term on annual contracts

questions_asked.question: Do you have SOC 2 Type II certification for data handling?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR compliant, can provide attestation

questions_asked.question: What's your typical legal review timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 5-7 business days for standard SaaS contracts

next_steps: Send final contract to Rowan for legal review
Target close within 2 weeks pending legal approval
